generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data for environmental perception and object detection in the autonomous vehicle system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor suite capturing real-time telemetry including motor, steering, and servo actuation states for vehicle state monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance MotorActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring motor actuation states for feedback in the adaptive cruise control system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorActuationData
]

instance SteeringActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring steering actuation states for feedback in the adaptive cruise control system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringActuationData
]

instance ServoActuatorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring servo actuation states for feedback in the adaptive cruise control system."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoActuationData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from LiDAR sensors processed via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking software component."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry data including motor, steering, and servo actuation states transmitted from the physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Data from motor actuation systems processed by the Actuation Functional Entity component."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuatorSensor
]

instance SteeringActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Data from steering actuation systems processed by the Actuation Functional Entity component."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringActuatorSensor
]

instance ServoActuationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Data from servo actuation systems processed by the Actuation Functional Entity component."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoActuatorSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component converting raw LiDAR points into structured object tracks and environmental representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state data including speed, position, and actuation states for ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, RealTimeVisualizationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks (e.g., lead vehicle, obstacles) derived from processed LiDAR point cloud data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, RealTimeVisualizationEnabler
]

instance AccLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Cloud-based computational model handling entire ACC system logic including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and distance-based control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CloudComputeAllocationEnabler
]

instance DrivingPathModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving path data for real-time visualization and ACC logic execution."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData, ObjectTrackModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeVisualizationEnabler, AccLogicModel
]

instance SystemPerformanceMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Performance metrics of the ACC system including latency, computation time, and control accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData, AccLogicModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataLoggingEnabler, RealTimeVisualizationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived vehicle state data for post-analysis and report generation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataLoggingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component aligning and formatting sensor data (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo) for downstream processing."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "User interface platform rendering 3D visualizations of vehicle state, object tracks, and driving paths."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance DataLoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Database system for storing and retrieving historical vehicle state data for post-analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance CloudComputeAllocationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Cloud-based infrastructure handling high-fidelity ACC computations, offloading from resource-constrained on-board ECUs."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance LowLatencyCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware ensuring real-time data synchronization between physical twin and DTS with 100ms latency for safety-critical operations."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance Ros2NetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network facilitating modular and interoperable communication between software components in the Digital Twin System."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central hub for serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions to ensure interoperability."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time visualization of live vehicle state data, object tracks, and system performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, HistoricalDataInsight, DrivingPathVisualizationInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing entire ACC system logic in the cloud, including lead vehicle detection, desired speed computation, and distance-based control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccLogicExecutionAction, ActuationFeedbackAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling operators to remotely enable/disable ACC, set desired speeds, and issue emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides AccControlAction, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live visualization of vehicle state including speed, position, and system performance metrics for situational awareness."
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Retrieved and generated reports of historical vehicle state data for post-analysis and debugging."
]

instance DrivingPathVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving path visualization with detected object tracks (e.g., lead vehicle, obstacles) for real-time monitoring."
]

instance AccLogicExecutionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Execution of ACC logic in the cloud including lead vehicle detection and desired speed computation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuationFeedbackAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmission of computed desired speed from DTS back to physical vehicle for real-time actuation (e.g., throttle, braking)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AccControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enabling/disabling of ACC and setting desired speeds via operator interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issuance of emergency braking commands remotely with low-latency constraints (100ms)."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity (Environment)
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with resource-constrained ECUs and advanced sensor/actuator technology for ACC."
    base:contains LiDARSensor, VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuatorSensor, SteeringActuatorSensor, ServoActuatorSensor
]

// C2: Digital Twin Entity (Environment)
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cloud-based digital representation of the physical twin handling data preprocessing, actuation, and device communication."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringModel, AccLogicModel, DataPreProcessingEnabler, CloudComputeAllocationEnabler,
        LowLatencyCommunicationEnabler, Ros2NetworkEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

// C16: User Entity (Environment)
instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User-facing components providing 3D visualization and control panel for operators and developers."
    base:contains RealTimeVisualizationEnabler, DataLoggingEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves the following control flows:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the **Actuation FE** component to control motor, steering, and servo systems based on processed digital twin data.
   - **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Commands**: Issued by the **Controlling FE** to adjust vehicle speed and path based on real-time environmental data (e.g., object tracks, projected driving paths).
   - **User Inputs**: Transmitted via the **User Interface FE** (e.g., control panel inputs) to override or adjust automated control systems.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Data Translation FE**: Serializes and formats control messages to ensure compatibility between the **Digital Twin Entity** (software) and **Physical Twin Entity** (actuators).
   - **ACC Controlling FE**: Generates structured control signals (e.g., throttle, steering angle adjustments) based on processed sensor data and user inputs.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - Not explicitly detailed in the document, but implied to rely on **ROS2 network security** (e.g., message authentication, data integrity checks) and **Wi-Fi network protocols** (e.g., UDP reliability mechanisms).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation Feedback Loop**: The **Actuation FE** receives confirmation of command execution from physical actuators (e.g., motor, steering servo) and relays this back to the digital twin for validation.
   - **Vehicle State Monitoring**: Real-time vehicle state data (e.g., motor RPM, steering angle) is continuously fed back to the digital twin for adaptive control adjustments.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Not explicitly described, but likely handled via **priority-based arbitration** in the **Controlling FE** (e.g., user inputs overriding automated ACC commands) or **ROS2 topic-based conflict resolution** (e.g., message queuing or priority queues).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - Implicit in the **Actuation FE** and **Controlling FE** workflows, where successful execution of commands (e.g., motor actuation, steering adjustments) is validated by comparing expected vs. actual vehicle state data.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination among devices. This temporal granularity is dictated by hardware limitations, though stricter synchronization (sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards for safety-critical vehicular applications. The system leverages **clock synchronization protocols** (unspecified but implied to be ROS2-compatible) to align time across distributed ROS2 nodes, ensuring deterministic behavior for services like sensor data fusion, actuation, and device communication. Latency requirements are implicitly constrained by the 100 ms synchronization window, with critical services (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) likely operating within this bound to maintain real-time fidelity between the physical twin (sensors/actuators) and digital twin (software components)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates its constellation through an **ISO 23247 RA-compliant yet customized architecture**, where software components are:
   - **Mapped to Functional Entities (FEs)** as ROS2 packages, with each package hosting one or more ROS2 nodes. This modularity enables **dynamic resource management** by isolating functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering* as a node within the Physical Twin Entity).
   - **Deployed on distributed hardware** (one entity per hardware component), requiring **ROS2-based inter-process communication** for coordination. The system’s orchestration relies on:
     - **Entity-based structuring**: Aligns with ISO 23247’s entity model but extends the Physical Twin Entity’s role to include general automotive functionality, altering ownership and engineering workflows.
     - **Selective FE adoption**: Not all ISO 23247 FEs are implemented; the *User Interface FE* is split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, while *Data Storage* lacks a direct FE alignment, necessitating ad-hoc integration.
     - **Cross-entity synchronization**: Achieved via ROS2’s publish-subscribe model, ensuring temporal and data consistency across the Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entities. The constellation’s resilience depends on this **decentralized yet protocol-driven coordination**."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247 Part 2** framework implicitly addresses **fidelity and validity** through its **entity-based architecture**, where the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** must accurately represent the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**. While the standard does not explicitly define **validation and verification methods**, it establishes a **closed-loop system** where the **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** ensures real-time synchronization between the physical and digital domains, reducing discrepancies. **Uncertainty sources** are not quantified, but the framework assumes that **data accuracy** is maintained via the **Data Collection Entity’s** direct interfacing with sensors/actuators. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are not specified, though the **User Entity** provides interfaces for monitoring, implying manual or automated oversight. **Quality assurance processes** are not detailed, but the **entity-based modularity** allows for **functional isolation**, enabling targeted validation of individual components (e.g., FEs). The standard does not prescribe **uncertainty quantification techniques** (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations, Bayesian inference) or **formal verification protocols** (e.g., model checking)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined as follows:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: Uses a **ROS2 network** for modular, interoperable communication between software components (e.g., **Data Collecting FE**, **Reporting FE**, **Controlling FE**).
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical-Digital)**: Relies on **Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer** for low-latency, high-throughput data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators) and **Digital Twin Entity** (software).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical Architecture**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Collects raw sensor data (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo) and transmits it via Wi-Fi.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts ROS2-based software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Controlling FE**, **Presentation FE**) for processing, control, and visualization.
     - **User Entity**: Interfaces with the digital twin via **User Interface FE** (e.g., 3D visualization, control panel).
   - **Data Flow**: Sensor data propagates from **Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity**, while control commands flow from **User/Digital Twin → Physical Twin**.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **LiDAR Point Cloud Data**: Requires **high bandwidth** for real-time transmission and processing (exact values not specified but implied to be low-latency for adaptive control).
   - **Actuation Commands**: Demand **low-latency** (<100ms implied for real-time control systems like ACC) to ensure timely response.
   - **Visualization Data**: Optimized via **pre-processing** to reduce bandwidth usage while maintaining real-time rendering.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **ROS2 Network**: Provides **modular redundancy** through topic-based communication, allowing failover to alternative data sources if primary feeds are disrupted.
   - **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: While UDP is connectionless, the system likely employs **retransmission logic** or **application-layer acknowledgments** to mitigate packet loss.
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a **single point of truth** for data serialization and unit conversion, reducing inconsistencies across the network.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - Not explicitly detailed, but implied to include:
     - **ROS2 Security**: Message authentication, encryption, and access control for software components.
     - **Wi-Fi Security**: Standard protocols (e.g., WPA3, VPN tunneling) for inter-entity communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied for **real-time processing** of sensor data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, vehicle state) to minimize latency. The **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Actuation FE**, and **Controlling FE** likely run on edge devices (e.g., onboard computers in the vehicle).
   - **On-Premise Deployment**: The **User Interface FE** (e.g., 3D visualization, control panel) is deployed locally to ensure low-latency interaction with the digital twin.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **Point Cloud Processing**: Requires **high-performance computing** (e.g., GPU acceleration) for real-time clustering and tracking of LiDAR data.
     - **Control Logic**: The **Controlling FE** (e.g., ACC) demands **low-latency processing** for adaptive control decisions.
     - **Data Translation**: Acts as a **lightweight but critical** component, handling serialization and unit conversion for all data streams.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Manages **historical data** (e.g., sensor logs, reports) with requirements for **scalable storage** (exact capacity not specified but implied to support long-term analytics).
     - **Real-Time Data**: Likely stored in **volatile memory** (e.g., RAM) for active processing, with periodic archiving to persistent storage.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular ROS2 Architecture**: Enables **horizontal scaling** by adding more nodes or processing units (e.g., additional LiDAR processing clusters).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a **scalable intermediary**, supporting dynamic addition of new data streams without redesigning the entire system.
   - **Wi-Fi/UDP Network**: Supports **multi-device connectivity**, allowing expansion to additional sensors or vehicles in a fleet scenario.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Hybrid Edge-On-Premise**: Combines edge processing (for real-time control) with on-premise visualization and user interaction.
   - **Containerized Deployment**: Implied by ROS2’s modular design, where components can be deployed as **Docker containers** or **ROS2 nodes** for portability and isolation.

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Redundant Data Paths**: ROS2’s topic-based communication allows **failover to alternative data sources** if primary feeds are lost.
   - **Real-Time Monitoring**: The **Data Monitoring** service ensures continuous validation of data integrity and system health.
   - **Fault Tolerance**: Critical components (e.g., **Controlling FE**, **Actuation FE**) likely implement **watchdog timers** or **heartbeat mechanisms** to detect and recover from failures.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Reference Architecture**: A modular framework where **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, **Digital Twin Entities**, and **User Entities** are standardized components.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The document references **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as a potential implementation method for **data exchange** between the **Data Collection Entity** and OMEs, ensuring **industrial interoperability**.
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: While **ISO 23247** is the primary standard, no additional **certification requirements** (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443) or **domain-specific specifications** (e.g., automotive, aerospace) are explicitly mentioned.
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of ISO 23247 (not detailed here) likely addresses **data formats and protocols**, but the provided context focuses on **Part 2’s architectural compliance** rather than granular interoperability standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, particularly in the context of **ISO 26262-1:2018 (Road vehicles – Functional safety)** and **ISO 15622:2018 (Intelligent Transport Systems – Adaptive Cruise Control Systems)**, incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** aligned with **automotive functional safety standards (ASIL levels)**. Key measures include:
  - **Cybersecurity protocols**: Integration with **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) security frameworks** as outlined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, which mandates **authentication, encryption (e.g., TLS 1.2/1.3), and secure communication channels** for real-time data exchange between vehicles and infrastructure.
  - **Access control mechanisms**: Role-based access with **multi-factor authentication (MFA)** for critical system components, ensuring only authorized entities (e.g., OEMs, regulatory bodies) interact with safety-relevant Digital Twin models.
  - **Threat mitigation strategies**: **Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS)** and **fail-safe mechanisms** (e.g., **watchdog timers, redundant system states**) to prevent unauthorized modifications to the Digital Twin’s **safety-critical parameters** (e.g., adaptive cruise control thresholds).
  - **Risk assessment approaches**: **ISO 26262-compliant Hazard Analysis and Risk Assessment (HARA)** to classify risks (e.g., **ASIL D for life-threatening failures**) and enforce **safety integrity levels (SIL)** in Digital Twin simulations.
  - **Authentication systems**: **Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)** for V2X communications, ensuring **non-repudiation** and **data integrity** in shared Digital Twin environments (per **3GPP TS 22.185**).
  - **Fail-safe mechanisms**: **Model-driven architecture (MDA)** for Digital Twin cockpits (as in **[37]**) enforces **state recovery protocols** (e.g., **rollback to last validated configuration**) upon detection of anomalies via **real-time monitoring tools** (e.g., **Siemens Polarion, IBM Engineering Lifecycle Tools**).
  - **Safety-critical system considerations**: **Formal verification methods** (e.g., **model checking with UPPAAL, SPIN**) are applied to validate Digital Twin behaviors against **ISO 26262 safety goals**, ensuring compliance with **automotive safety lifecycle processes (ASPICE)**."
]
